hook.text: The code worked in dev but broke in production. The environments were totally different. Classic mistake.

buildup.text: A staging environment mirrors production as closely as possible: same OS, same configs, same data shape.

discovery.text: The pipeline deploys to staging first. Automated smoke tests verify everything works before production.

twist.text: Staging isn't perfect. Some bugs only appear under real traffic. That's why canary releases exist.

climax.text: Treat staging like production. If you treat it as a dumping ground, it won't catch real issues.

punchline.text: Staging catches production problems early. Mirror everything.

quiz.question: Why should staging mirror production closely?
options: To catch environment-specific bugs before they reach users
To save money on production servers
